### Context

responses.mistral_7b_instruct:
The changes made in Example 1 (Combined) involve a modification to the return type of the function `add` in the `ToStringTuple.cpp` file from `std::size_t` to `std::pair<std::size_t, std::ostringstream*>`.

Here is the simplified srcML showing the structural change:

```xml
<function>
  <type>
    <diff:delete>std::size_t</diff:delete>
    <diff:insert>std::pair<std::size_t, std::ostringstream*></diff:insert>
  </type>
  <name>add</name><parameter_list>()</parameter_list> { ... }
</function>
```

- `<diff:delete>`: What was removed (return type changed FROM this)
- `<diff:insert>`: What was added (return type changed TO this)
- This shows function signature evolved to return more information.

The commit message should be "Fix double-to-float conversion warning in tests".

responses.codeqwen_7b_chat:
srcML extractions: <function> <diff:delete>std::size_t</diff:delete> <diff:insert>std::pair<std::size_t, std::ostringstream*>></diff:insert>  { ... } </function>

Interpretation: The commit message is in XML format and describes the change made to the function signature. The change involves modifying the return type from `std::size_t` to a more complex data structure - `std::pair<std::size_t, std::ostringstream*>>`. This indicates that the function now returns a pair consisting of two elements: the size_t value and a pointer to an output string stream. The commit message provides an essential detail about the changes made to the function signature. It is important to note that the change has a significant impact on the function's behavior and its return type, which may affect other parts of the codebase that depend on this function.
